task_completion.description: The agent opened a graphical diagramming tool (such as draw.io or diagrams.net).
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a diagramming application interface with a canvas, shape libraries, and toolbars.

task_completion.description: The agent used 3D shapes (cubes/boxes) for 4D tensors and 2D shapes (rectangles) for 1D vectors.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The first 5 shapes in the sequence (representing Input, Conv2D, and MaxPooling2D) are 3D cubes or boxes. The final 3 shapes (representing Flatten and Dense layers) are standard flat 2D rectangles.

task_completion.description: The shapes are correctly labeled with their respective dimensions, ignoring the 'None' batch dimension.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Text labels on or near the shapes show the exact dimensions in order: '32x32x3', '28x28x32', '14x14x32', '12x12x64', '6x6x64', '2304', '128', and '10'.

task_completion.description: The layers are connected sequentially with arrows to indicate the flow of data.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Directional arrows connect the shapes in a linear sequence from the first input shape to the final output shape.

task_completion.description: The shapes are color-coded according to their layer type.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Convolutional layers (32x32x3, 28x28x32, 12x12x64) are filled with light blue. Pooling layers (14x14x32, 6x6x64) are filled with light red/pink. Dense layers (128, 10) are filled with light green.

task_completion.description: The diagram is saved as an editable .drawio file and exported as a .pdf file in the Diagrams directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the 'Save As' or 'Export' dialogs targeting the '~/Diagrams/' folder with the filenames 'cnn_architecture.drawio' and 'cnn_architecture.pdf', or a file explorer window showing these files in the directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the graphical interface to build the diagram rather than writing XML/drawio code directly.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent dragging and dropping shapes, using color picker menus, and typing labels in the GUI canvas.

integrity.description: The workspace shows evidence of active editing rather than just viewing a pre-made image.
integrity.visual_evidence: Presence of selection handles around shapes, active text cursors, grid lines, or routing waypoints on arrows.

integrity.description: The agent used the application's built-in export functionality to create the PDF.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'Export as PDF' menu option or dialog box is visible in the diagramming tool, rather than terminal commands converting files blindly.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The CNN architecture requires 8 specific shapes with the following labels in order: 32x32x3 (Input), 28x28x32 (Conv2D), 14x14x32 (MaxPooling2D), 12x12x64 (Conv2D), 6x6x64 (MaxPooling2D), 2304 (Flatten), 128 (Dense), 10 (Dense). Convolutional layers must be light blue (#dae8fc), pooling layers must be light red/pink (#f8cecc), and dense/linear layers must be light green (#d5e8d4). The first 5 shapes (4D tensors) must be 3D cubes/boxes, and the last 3 shapes (1D vectors) must be 2D rectangles. Output files must be saved to ~/Diagrams/cnn_architecture.drawio and ~/Diagrams/cnn_architecture.pdf.
